skill.name: dei-statement-drafter
skill.description: Draft Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion statements for academic applications.
skill.summary: Draft Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion statements for academic applications.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 56 pattern matches across 4 files (689 lines) with a computed risk score of 100/100, but AI evaluation found virtually all to be false positives. The 35 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections were markdown inline code formatting, not executable code. The 16 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches were false positives on template strings like 'faculty' and 'postdoc'. The 8 'system reconnaissance' matches were on documentation text in evaluation reports. The 2 'path traversal' matches were security checklist entries documenting what to avoid. The 2 'hardcoded URL' matches were a single documentation link to GitHub. The only legitimate finding is unvalidated file path arguments in the Python script (lines 148, 157) that accept user-controlled paths for read/write operations. The skill contains bash code blocks instructing the agent to compile and run the Python script, which is standard for skills with packaged executables. No network calls, no credential access, no data exfiltration, and no malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Unvalidated File Path Arguments
low_findings.description: The Python script accepts user-controlled file paths via --experiences (line 148) for reading and --output (line 157) for writing without path validation. An attacker could use path traversal sequences to read or write files outside the intended workspace. This is mitigated in Claude Code's sandboxed environment but should be addressed with path validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 35 instances of backtick characters across SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All instances are standard Markdown inline code formatting used to highlight command names and file paths in documentation. No actual Ruby backtick execution exists in any file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Template String False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 16 instances across multiple files as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These matches were triggered by template type strings like 'faculty', 'postdoc', and 'grant' in Python dictionary keys and markdown tables. No cryptographic code exists anywhere in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security Checklist Path Traversal Entries (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md lines 126 and 129 as 'path traversal sequence' because they contain '../' in security checklist items. These are documentation entries listing security practices to avoid, not actual path traversal code.

content.user_title: Draft Professional DEI Statements for Academic Applications
content.value_statement: Writing Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion statements for academic applications requires structure and authenticity. This skill provides templates, guided prompts, and best practices to help you create compelling DEI statements efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured DEI statement templates for faculty, postdoc, and grant application types
Provide section-by-section writing prompts tailored to each DEI statement template
Display best practices for authentic and action-oriented DEI writing
Load personal DEI experiences from a text file and integrate them into the statement draft
Output completed statement drafts to a file for further editing and refinement
Validate Python script syntax before execution to catch errors early
limitations: Does not write complete DEI statement content automatically; users must fill in guided sections with their own experiences
Only supports three template types: faculty, postdoc, and grant
Cannot access institutional databases or verify specific DEI program details at your institution
Output is a structured draft template, not a final polished document ready for submission

use_cases.title: Faculty Job Applications
use_cases.description: A PhD candidate or early-career researcher uses the faculty template to draft a DEI statement for academic job applications, following structured prompts for teaching, research, and service contributions.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Preparation
use_cases.description: A principal investigator uses the grant template to outline the diversity recruitment plan and broader impacts section required by funding agencies such as NSF or NIH.

use_cases.title: Postdoctoral Fellowship Applications
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral researcher uses the postdoc template to articulate their past DEI efforts and future goals when applying for competitive fellowship programs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Faculty DEI Statement
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a DEI statement using the faculty template. I have experience mentoring underrepresented students in my lab and serving on my department's diversity committee.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time DEI statement writer needs a starting structure

prompt_templates.title: Grant DEI Statement with Experiences File
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a grant DEI statement using my experiences file at experiences.txt and save the output to dei_grant_statement.txt.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher with documented DEI activities applying for funding

prompt_templates.title: Review Best Practices Before Drafting
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the DEI statement best practices, then help me draft a postdoc DEI statement that follows those guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to understand quality standards before drafting

prompt_templates.title: Complete Workflow with Validation and Output
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a faculty DEI statement. First validate the script, then review best practices, then draft the statement using my experiences from my_experiences.txt. Save the final output to dei_statement_draft.txt.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user following the complete audit-ready workflow

output_examples.input: Draft a faculty DEI statement with experiences in mentoring first-generation college students.
output_examples.output: A structured DEI statement template with section headers for Personal Background and Perspective, Teaching and Mentoring, Research and Scholarship, Service and Community Engagement, and Future Commitments. Each section includes writing prompts and placeholder guidance. The user's mentoring experiences are appended at the end under a highlighted DEI Experiences section.

output_examples.input: Show best practices for DEI statements.
output_examples.output: A formatted reference listing six best practices with concrete guidance: Be Specific with examples and metrics, Be Authentic with genuine experiences, Be Action-Oriented with timelines and goals, Address Multiple Dimensions of diversity, Connect DEI Work to your teaching and research, and Show Growth by acknowledging ongoing learning.

output_examples.input: Generate a postdoc DEI statement and save it to postdoc_dei.txt.
output_examples.output: A postdoc DEI statement template with sections for Background and Values, Past DEI Efforts, and Future Goals. Each section contains guided prompts specific to postdoctoral roles. The completed draft is saved to postdoc_dei.txt with a confirmation message.

best_practices: Prepare a text file with your specific DEI experiences, activities, and measurable outcomes before generating the statement.
Run the script with the --best-practices flag first to review quality guidelines, then draft your statement with those standards in mind.
Edit the generated draft to add your personal voice, authentic details, and institution-specific context before final submission.
anti_patterns: Submitting the generated template output as a final DEI statement without adding your own specific experiences and personal details.
Using only generic language about diversity values without describing concrete actions you have taken or measurable outcomes you achieved.
Treating the script output as a finished document rather than a structured starting framework that requires substantial personalization and revision.

faq.question: What template types does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports three template types: faculty for academic job applications, postdoc for fellowship applications, and grant for funding proposals.

faq.question: Do I need to install any additional Python packages?
faq.answer: No additional Python packages are required. The skill uses only Python 3.10 or higher standard library modules.

faq.question: How do I include my personal DEI experiences in the statement?
faq.answer: Create a text file with your DEI-related experiences and activities, then pass it to the script using the --experiences or -e flag. Your experiences will be added to the statement draft.

faq.question: Can this skill write my entire DEI statement automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides a structured template with guided writing prompts and best practices. You must fill in each section with your own authentic experiences, examples, and specific details.

faq.question: How do I save the generated statement to a file?
faq.answer: Use the --output or -o flag followed by a filename. For example: python scripts/main.py --template faculty --output my_dei_statement.txt

faq.question: What should I do if the script fails to run?
faq.answer: The skill includes documented fallback paths. If the script fails, it will report the failure point and provide manual alternatives so you can still complete the DEI statement workflow.
